seoTitle: How to Add or Paste Raw JSON Into n8n
metaDescription: Learn how to switch a node's input mode to raw JSON in n8n so you can paste structured data directly into your workflow canvas.
intro: Sometimes it's faster to paste structured JSON directly into n8n rather than building it field by field, and the platform supports that through a toggle available on nodes like Edit Fields. This guide covers switching to raw JSON mode, pasting your data in, and checking that it parses correctly.

steps.heading: Add an Edit Fields or Code node
steps.body: Place one of these nodes onto your canvas depending on how you want to work with the JSON.

steps.heading: Switch to JSON input mode
steps.body: Find the toggle inside the node's settings that switches from individual fields to a raw JSON text box.

steps.heading: Paste your JSON data
steps.body: Enter your structured JSON object or array directly into the text field.

steps.heading: Check for formatting errors
steps.body: Review the data preview pane for any syntax issues before moving on.

steps.heading: Connect it downstream
steps.body: Route the parsed JSON output into your next workflow steps once it looks correct.

faq.question: Which n8n nodes let me paste raw JSON?
faq.answer: The Edit Fields (Set) node and the Code node both support entering or working with raw JSON data.

faq.question: Why would I paste JSON instead of building fields manually?
faq.answer: It's often faster when you already have structured data ready and don't want to recreate it field by field.

faq.question: How do I know if my pasted JSON is valid?
faq.answer: The data preview pane will typically flag formatting issues if your JSON doesn't parse correctly.

faq.question: Can I use pasted JSON as mock data for testing?
faq.answer: Yes, pasting static JSON is a common way to test workflow logic before connecting a live data source.
